title: scene image generation specification
scope: fixed delivery contract for reusable location images and shot reference frames
core_decisions: Not every location requires separate close, medium and wide images.
Not every location requires multi-angle or multi-direction images.
Reusable secondary locations require at least one 9:16 location board.
Core recurring locations require a board plus wide, mid and close production frames.
Complex action locations require reverse or side angles, route maps, lighting variants and shot-specific reference frames.

content_rules: 3 to 6 local set zones or detail callouts in one vertical design board
show main space, entrance or exit, key props, material close-up, lighting mood and occupation or historical overlay
no readable labels, no watermark, no UI and no modern graphic arrows

image_types.purpose: lock whole-space identity and first-read geography
content_rules: vertical establishing frame, not horizontal panorama
show spatial hierarchy, terrain pressure, entrance or exit and local scale
use anonymous silhouettes only when needed for scale

image_types.purpose: lock action blocking, camera axis and character movement paths
content_rules: show doors, wells, tables, ship rails, tunnel mouths, wall cracks or pursuit directions
allow faceless scale figures or backs unless this is a specific shot frame
preserve the same location anchors as the board

image_types.purpose: lock material, prop and institutional trace details
content_rules: focus on wax seals, grain tokens, erased emblems, bone bells, steam valves, rope, mud, ash or tool marks
1:1 texture studies are allowed only as texture library references, not direct video frames
detail must match the location palette and material bible

image_types.purpose: prevent spatial drift in multi-shot editing
content_rules: same location from reverse, side, entrance-to-exit or exit-to-entrance angle
camera direction changes but anchors, color, material and occupation traces remain stable
required only when motion, turning, pursuit, confrontation or cross-shot geography matters

image_types.purpose: lock visible story-state changes in the same location
content_rules: same or nearly same camera as the base production frame
use only for story-relevant states such as fire, ash, snow, night, river fog or Qingming white light

image_types.purpose: support director, storyboard and prompt-room spatial reasoning
content_rules: show entrances, exits, route, obstacles and upper/lower level relationships
not used directly as a video frame
allowed to use simplified markers for planning

image_types.purpose: serve as exact first frame, last frame or key frame for video generation
content_rules: single complete video frame, never a collage
no text, labels, UI or watermark
must match director-room shot id, camera, blocking, props, lighting and continuity locks

coverage_tiers.rule: If generation-plan marks the shot as T2V with no stable character or location requirement, no scene image is required.
Add mid_staging when the location carries concrete action blocking.

applies_to: location repeatedly reused in shot lists
coverage_tiers.rule: Close, mid and wide production frames are required because multiple future shots will reuse the space.
Generate shot_reference_frame images according to director-room shot-list when production starts.

multi_angle_required_when: camera or characters move from entrance to exit
characters turn around, look back, chase, confront or cross the space
same location appears across multiple shots, scenes or episodes
spatial anchors must remain fixed, such as well-to-medicine-house, ferry-deck-to-engine-hatch, tunnel-fork-to-stone-door, corridor-to-honeycomb-cells
director-room marks the shot as I2V, FLF2V or REDRAW and spatial continuity matters

id_rules: major revisions use v02, v03 or later instead of overwriting old files
failed or drifting images go under assets/rejected/
